free_text: Total neutron yield from the reactions 13C(alpha,n)16O
Neutron yield curve given
ORNL 5.5-MV Van de Graaff
Graphite-sphere neutron detector
Normalized to known 180 cross sections.
Bombarding energy calibrated using
- 19F(a,n)22Na resonance at 2609 keV, and
- 7Li(a,n)10B reaction threshold at 4380 keV,
together with previous results of Bair et al. (1962)
for 180(a,n)21Ne which were based on the 7Li(p,n)7Be
threshold
Previous 18O(a,n)21Ne measurement
Energies corrected for target thickness.

On. REFERENCE type: R->P for ORNL-4659
OS. Data corrections in sub.5
Acetylene on Pt backing, enriched in 13C.
Target thickness ~5 keV at 1.057-MeV resonance.
Based on thick target measurements made on 1.057-MeV
resonance with both enriched and natural carbon, in
conjunction with thin target measurements made over
1.057-MeV resonance and wide resonance at 2.4 MeV.
The cross-section scale is based on the
thick target measurements as discussed above and are
estimated to be reliable to 20% for structure
suitably wider than the target thickness.
By Gerry Hale (2014-10-16) and compiler (2014-10-20):
Correction factor 0.80 to 0.85 mentioned in the
"Note added in proof" of Phys.Rev.C7(1973)1356 is not
applied to the data set compiled in this EXFOR entry.
Author's tabulated data, corresponding to Fig.3
of Phys.Rev.C7,1356,1973, as received through G.Hale
(LANL)
Tabulated data originally received by
Gerry Hale (LANL) from Duane Larson (ORNL) around 1997
Replaced CM NACRE data with author's
tabulated data
On. COMMENT added.
Targets made by bombarding tantalum blank with 24-keV
170 ions in a magnetic separator. Feed gas enriched
Target thickness is about 36 keV at 1 MeV.
Measurements made with thin anodized O-18 - O-18
targets (on the same cycle of the analyzing magnet)
gave the experimental widths of the 1.96- and 2.03-MeV
resonances as 5.0 and 2.5 keV, respectively,
and their separation as 63.5 keV.
The cross section scale has been determined by
normalizing to the O-18 resonances at 2.56 and 2.71 MeV
and taking into account the known ration of O-17 to
O-18 enrichment.
The data have not been corrected for the variation of
graphite sphere detector efficiency as a function of
neutron energy since the energy spectrum of the
neutrons is unknown. This correction would be
negligible at the lower energies but might be as much
as 5% at highest bombarding energies.
over-all error is estimated as 25% excluding
uncertainty of graphite sphere detector efficiency.

Target made by anodizing a tantalum blank at 22 V in
water enriched to 97 at. % 180. Target thickness
was 13 keV for 2.5-MeV alpha particles.
The cross-section scale has been obtained
by normalizing to the older data, the estimate of the
error remains 25%.
Fig. 7 of Phys.Rev. C7, 1356.
